poem:
**Forty-Nine Days**

He backed her, then un-backed her—  
a vote you can't unspend.  

At Hay, among the hardbacks,  
regret is turned to paperback:  
"Biggest mistake."  
No friends in that direction.  

Forty-nine days of weather—  
a bright forecast, a broken barometer.  

"Good riddance," he says, and shrugs:  
the ballot box is a mirror;  
we choose the face that looks back.
prompt: You are the poet Simon Armitage. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "Sajid Javid says backing Liz Truss to lead Tories was his ‘biggest political mistake’ Sajid Javid said that supporting Liz Truss in the Conservative leadership contest that ultimately made her prime minister was his “biggest mistake in politics”. Speaking at the Hay festival in Wales while promoting his memoir, the former chancellor, who is no longer an MP, said there were friends in the Conservative party he remained in contact with. Asked whether he still speaks to Truss, Javid responded: “No. I said ‘friends’.” Broadcaster Aasmah Mir then asked: “Hang on a minute, did you not back her in the first leadership election?” to which Javid replied: “Biggest mistake in politics.” After Boris Johnson’s resignation in 2022, Javid initially launched his own campaign for the Conservative leadership before backing Truss after being eliminated from the contest. Truss’s premiership lasted just 49 days in 2022 after her government’s disastrous mini-budget. Javid, who stood down as an MP at the 2024 election, was also asked by Mir what he thought of the swathe of Tory MPs defecting to Reform, to which he responded simply: “Good riddance.” He also argued that the quality of Britain’s political class had deteriorated in recent years. He said: “Has the calibre of politicians declined in recent years? Yes. I think dramatically. But it would be unfair … to blame that just on the politicians. The politicians reflect what you vote for. “Ultimately, the calibre of the politicians is on the people. If you want different politicians, you’ve". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
